{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "lobuhi", "type": "reporter"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37011545?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lobuhi/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lobuhi/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lobuhi/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lobuhi/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/lobuhi", "id": 37011545, "login": "lobuhi", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjM3MDExNTQ1", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lobuhi/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lobuhi/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lobuhi/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lobuhi/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lobuhi/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/lobuhi", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-59818", "cvss": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": [], "cwes": [], "description": "### Impact
_What kind of vulnerability is it? Who is impacted?_

When etcd is configured with `--listen-client-http-urls` to split the HTTP and gRPC client endpoints onto separate listeners, the `--client-crl-file` Certificate Revocation List is not enforced on the gRPC listener. A client presenting a revoked certificate is correctly rejected by the HTTP endpoint but continues to authenticate successfully over gRPC.

### Patches
_Has the problem been patched? What versions should users upgrade to?_

This vulnerability is patched in the following versions:

- etcd 3.6.13
- etcd 3.5.32

### Workarounds
_Is there a way for users to fix or remediate the vulnerability without upgrading?_

If upgrading is not immediately possible, reduce exposure by restricting network access to etcd client ports using firewall rules or network policy so that only trusted components can reach the server. This limits the pool of clients that could exploit a revoked certificate but does not enforce CRL revocation.

### Reporter

Luis Toro ([@lobuhi](https://github.com/lobuhi) on Github)

### References
_Are there any links users can visit to find out more?_

title: etcd: CRL enforcement bypass on gRPC listener when `--listen-client-http-urls` is configured
